extraction.one_line_summary: DENZ Nico won from a 30-rider breakaway after surviving near-elimination on Col Braida, then attacking with 12km to go before holding off sprint challengers.
tactical_patterns: 30-rider breakaway stayed clear on transition stage before mountains, revealing GC teams prioritized recovery over controlling stage outcomes.
Denz attacked 12km from finish on final descent, forcing chase collaboration breakdown rather than waiting for inevitable sprint.
Five-rider split from 30-man break failed because batistello illness prevented rotation, showing numerical advantage trumps small gaps with sick riders.

rider_signals.DENZ Nico: positive: nearly dropped on Col Braida but recovered to attack solo with 12km remaining, suggesting superior recovery capacity under duress
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: abandoned due to illness without reaching back of peloton, indicating week-two fatigue vulnerability for sprinters
rider_signals.BATISTELLO Samuel: negative: dropped from 5-rider split despite initial selection, illness preventing him from capitalizing on breakaway position
